variants: Keva Klopp was born in Blackberry Hollow, a quaint little town known for its charming landscapes. Currently, Keva resides in Sunnybrook Arcade, an innovative community that inspires creativity. Renowned for her inventive spirit, she was honored with the prestigious Dewhaven Award for her outstanding contributions to technology. One of her most significant works is the Thundertide Conductor Module, a device that revolutionized energy transfer systems. In her leisure time, Keva belongs to the Desert Glass Walkers, a club dedicated to exploring the artistry of glasswork in desert settings.

With each endeavor, Keva continues to leave a significant mark on both the technical and artistic communities.
